testModifications.reasoning: No test modifications detected. The test file was created by the test-writer session and not altered.

criteria.criterion: AC-1: TicketEventService.create(data) writes to TicketEvent and returns the created record
AC-2: AgentEventService.create(data) writes to AgentEvent and returns the created record
AC-3: DecisionEventService.create(data) writes to DecisionEvent and returns the created record
AC-4: All three services are called by KodaDomainWriter after relevant operations
AC-5: Event creation is included in the WriteResult.provenance output
AC-6: Creating an event with a non-existent projectId throws ForbiddenError with code PROJECT_NOT_FOUND
AC-7: ActorResolver maps current Koda auth and agent context into the Phase 1 actor model before permission checks run
AC-8: Only actors with role admin, developer, or agent on the target project can call event write operations, and GET /admin/outbox requires admin

reasoning: All 28 story-scoped tests pass. Implementation follows project conventions: services use constructor injection with typed PrismaService, ForbiddenAppException is thrown for invalid projectId, ActorResolver correctly maps user/agent context to Phase 1 actor model, KodaDomainWriter includes provenance in WriteResult, and role-based access control enforces admin/developer/agent roles. The three event services (TicketEventService, AgentEventService, DecisionEventService) are properly wired in EventsModule and KodaDomainWriter calls them in sequence after domain operations. Code is clean with no any types, proper error handling, and follows nathapp-nestjs-patterns.
